programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: Amelia Dyer was perhaps the most prolific killer in British history. She earned a living through murder. And her victims were babies.

programme.title: Murder Maps: In The Shadow Of Jack
programme.desc: At the time of Jack The Ripper, London was home to some of the most terrible individuals the city has ever seen. One of the very worst was the elusive Borough Poisoner, George Chapman.

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: It was a crime which shocked Britain. In 1878, a widow was killed in her own home, the killer was her own servant. For the young servant did not only kill and dismember her employer – she stole her clothes, her property and her identity.

programme.title: Murder Maps: The Lady Killer
programme.desc: As Europe recovered from the damage of WWII, soldiers made their way home from the front line. But one man, brought back all the savagery and brutality of the fighting.

programme.title: Murder Maps: The Blackout Ripper
programme.desc: It was WWII and London was rocked by the Blitz from the planes flying above. But down on the streets below another terrible danger lurked in the shadows. A serial killer was on the loose.

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: In 1911, a gun battle on the streets of London, The Siege of Sidney Street the first breaking-news story in history.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: MANMADE
programme.desc: When it comes to disasters, mother nature has mankind beat. However, as mankind expands, evolves and technology advances, it comes with dangerous challenges. When errors are made or unforeseen circumstances occur, the results can be devastating.

programme.title: Wonders: What's the Universe Made Of?
programme.desc: NOVA Wonders journeys to the stars and back to investigate what we know and don’t know about the universe. Find out how scientists are discovering new secrets about the history of the universe and why they’re predicting a shocking future.

programme.title: scienceNOW: Can I Eat That?
programme.desc: What are the scientific secrets behind your favorite foods? David Pogue ventures into labs and kitchens to uncover the hidden truths behind the flavors and textures we take for granted every day.

programme.title: scienceNOW: How Smart are Animals?
programme.desc: Hosted by Neil deGrasse Tyson, examine these topics: Dog Genius; Creative Dolphins; Octopus & Cuttlefish; and a profile of scientist Irene Pepperberg, who has revolutionized scientists’ ideas about animal communication and intelligence.

programme.title: scienceNOW: What Will the Future Be Like?
programme.desc: The technologies that will transform our lives decades from now are already taking shape in laboratories around the world. Segments include: Humanoid Robots; Mind-Reading Machines; Augmented Reality; Profile - Adrien Treuille.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Fermi Paradox
programme.desc: The Fermi Paradox asks the ultimate question: Given a few reasonable assumptions about the chance of life arising elsewhere in the universe and the distance between the stars, why has an alien civilization not presented itself?

programme.title: The New Frontier: Martian Attractions
programme.desc: The Latest in Martian exploration from orbit and on the ground, new sources of water and planetary dust storms are a few of the stories unfolding on to the Red Planet.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Getting There
programme.desc: Launching payloads into space is a very difficult task, historically rocket launches are the proverbial 'swords into ploughshares'.

programme.title: The New Frontier: First Light
programme.desc: For an instrument first developed as recently as the 17th century, the telescope has travelled a long way.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Virtual Universe
programme.desc: Our understanding of the cosmos is limited by the amount of time we have to see it. Fortunately, computer simulations can help us see how galaxies interact over millions of years, and even how dark matter structures the universe.

programme.title: RISING TIDES
programme.desc: The next generation of Earth observation satellites are now in place maintaining the continuity of long-term climate data, in particular the planets rising temperatures and related ocean levels.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Stars Above
programme.desc: Stars are a bit like human beings. They can be warm or cold, they come in all kinds of shapes and sizes.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Superscopes
programme.desc: The human eye can only see visible light, a small spectrum of electromagnetic waves. Today, we have instruments that let us perceive all wavelengths.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Cosmic Rays
programme.desc: Cosmic rays can tell us much about the universe. They are not simply another form of light, but carriers of information about powerful and unseen processes at work thousands or even millions of light years away.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Water Worlds
programme.desc: The key ingredient to life in the universe is water. Until recently we thought Earth was the only place in the solar system to have any water at all. Now that probes are peering around nearby worlds we are finding that is just not the case.

programme.title: Super Mario Brothers Super Show: Bad Rap
programme.desc: Mario, Luigi, Princess Toadstool and Toad have arrived in Rap Land, because King Koopa had kidnapped Rap Land's king, and is attempting to take everyone's money by playing an eerie theme throughout the land.

programme.title: Super Mario Brothers Super Show: Robo Koopa
programme.desc: Mario, Luigi, Princess Toadstool and Toad have ventured to Robo Land in pursuit of King Koopa, whom they had intended to stop.

programme.title: Super Mario Brothers Super Show: The Fire of Hercufleas
programme.desc: In the ancient city of Mythis, Mario, Luigi, Toad and Princess Toadstool are searching for a legendary hero named Hercufleas, who is an old friend of Toad's.

programme.title: Super Mario Bros 3 Adventures: Do The Koopa/Kootie Pie Rocks
programme.desc: Mario and his friends set out for Darkland and the Temple of Gloom./Mario, Luigi and the Princess go to the real world to see Milli Vanilli in concert.

programme.title: Super Mario Bros 3 Adventures: Princess Toadstool For President/Never Koop A Koopa
programme.desc: Princess Toadstool is fed up with Koopa and challenges him to try and win an election./Koopa announces that he is giving up being bad for good!

programme.title: Super Mario Brothers Super Show: Mario Meets Koop-Zilla
programme.desc: In the oriental city of Sayonara, Mario, Toad, Princess Toadstool and Luigi, who is pulling the cart Mario, Toad and Princess Toadstool are riding, are searching for a weapon that can help them defeat King Koopa, Super Sushi.

programme.title: Super Mario Brothers Super Show: Bonkers from Yonkers
programme.desc: Mario and Co head to the Linguini Empire to attend a benefit spaghetti dinner. When they arrive, Princess and Toad are separated from the Marios and meet Koopa, who's taken over.

programme.title: Super Mario Brothers Super Show: Too Hot to Handle
programme.desc: In Lava Land, Mario, Luigi, Toad and Princess Toadstool using a variety of floating rafts to reach an island named WakiWaki in the middle of the ocean

programme.title: Super Mario Brothers Super Show: Toad Warriors
programme.desc: In the badlands of Car Land, Mario, Luigi, Princess Toadstool and Toad are riding in a brown convertible car, planning on rendezvousing with several Mushroom rebels.

programme.title: Super Mario Brothers Super Show: The Pied Koopa
programme.desc: In Pasta Land, King Koopa uses a magical flute to lure all of the local Mushroom children away from the town, and leads them to his nearby castle.

programme.title: Super Mario Brothers Super Show: The Bird! The Bird!
programme.desc: While venturing in a snowy world, Toad is kidnapped by a nearsighted Birdo who is convinced that he is her missing baby.

programme.title: Sky Kids
programme.desc: As a pair of schoolboys sneak a look around an empty private aircraft, two criminals take off with the kids still inside. Upon discovering the men are dangerous gangsters, the horrified lads inadvertently become embroiled in the mobsters's scheme.

programme.title: Bottle Shock
programme.desc: The story of the early days of California wine making featuring the now infamous, blind Paris wine tasting of 1976 that has come to be known as "Judgment of Paris". Feat: Chris Pine, Alan Rickman & Bill Pullman.

programme.title: Noah
programme.desc: Russell Crowe stars as Noah in the timeless story of courage, sacrifice, and hope. This visually stunning, action-adventure is hailed as "one of the most unforgettable Biblical epics ever put on film." *Richard Roeper, Chicago-Sun-Times

programme.title: World Poker Tour: Borgata Poker Open - Part 2
programme.desc: Five players compete at Borgata Hotel Casino & Spa for the more than $900,000 first-place prize.

programme.title: World Poker Tour: World Poker Finals - Part 3
programme.desc: Three players compete at Foxwoods Resort and Casino for the $450,000 first-place prize.

programme.title: Bellagio 5 Diamond World Poker Classic
programme.desc: Six players compete in the prestigious WPT Five Diamond World Poker Classic at Bellagio for the more than $800,000 first-place prize.

programme.title: The Pink Panther
programme.desc: Comic icon Steve Martin steps into the shoes of the late Peter Sellers and wonderfully reprises the role of the bumbling, world famous Inspector Jacques Clouseau in this remake of Blake Edwards comedy classic, The Pink Panther.

programme.title: Invasion of the Body Snatchers
programme.desc: People accuse their loved ones of being emotionless impostors. At first, Dr. Bennell tries to convince them they’re wrong, but they’re not. Plant-like extraterrestrials have invaded Earth, taking possession of their souls while they sleep.

programme.title: Battle Beyond The Stars
programme.desc: A young farmer sets out to recruit mercenaries to defend his peaceful planet, which is under threat of invasion by the evil tyrant Sador and his armada of aggressors. Starring: George Peppard, Robert Vaughn, Richard Thomas

programme.title: Union Bound
programme.desc: Union Bound is a thrilling Civil War film that shows the strength and courage of the human spirit. Sergeant Joseph Hoover went to war to preserve the Union but finds himself captured and sent to a prisoner of war camp.

programme.title: Hercules and the Captive Women
programme.desc: Hercules saves a beautiful maiden from the hands of an evil creature and she takes him to her home, Atlantis.

programme.title: The Librarian: Quest for the Spear
programme.desc: As a means of finding the legendary book known as the Key of Solomon, Flynn pairs with a brainy archaeologist to first acquire the map legend. Unfortunately, they must elude the sinister General Samir and the ulterior plans of Flynn's uncle.

programme.title: Last Chance Café
programme.desc: After uncovering evidence that her stepfather was murdered by a ring of corrupt cops and judges, including her DA ex-husband, a woman is forced to flee with her daughter and hide in a small farming town.

programme.title: Twin Betrayal
programme.desc: Hoping for a fresh start, Julia, a troubled woman, moves to Los Angeles with her new boyfriend. After settling into a new home, mysterious things begin to happen. It's not long before Julia becomes convinced that her demons have moved with her.

programme.title: Full Exposure: The Sex Tapes Scandal
programme.desc: A high-priced call girl (Vanessa Williams) who has been secretly videotaping her rich clients is murdered.

programme.title: Like Mother, Like Daughter
programme.desc: Starring Dani Kind and Michelle Stafford. When Dawna Williams' college-aged daughter goes missing, she investigates her daughter's disappearance herself, unaware that her biggest supporter is also the man who may have murdered her child.

programme.title: Attack Of The Super Monsters
programme.desc: In the year 2000 the dinosaurs reappear on planet Earth, terrorizing mankind.

programme.title: Breaking Infinity
programme.desc: A scientific researcher who has become unstuck in time must figure out what happened to him and stop the coming end of the world.

programme.title: In Time
programme.desc: Justin Timberlake and Amanda Seyfried co-star in this sci-fi thriller set in a future world where aging stops at 25, and the only way for one man to stay alive is to steal more time.

programme.title: Deadliest Journeys: Congo Katanga
programme.desc: In Katanga, many people are waiting for a train named after a bird, the “Swallow”. More than 2,000 people, three times the train’s capacity, are squeezing in the carriages.

programme.title: Deadliest Journeys: Ethiopia
programme.desc: Travel on the oldest and most dangerous train in the world as it battles derailments and landslides while snaking through Ethiopia.

programme.title: Deadliest Journeys: Congo Katanga
programme.desc: In Katanga, many people are waiting for a train named after a bird, the “Swallow”. More than 2,000 people, three times the train’s capacity, are squeezing in the carriages.

programme.title: Timber Kings: Active Duty
programme.desc: In California, Joel is under the gun, with a job that’s behind schedule before it even begins. Meanwhile, Beat marshals the troops to finish a mega project on the verge of shipping out.

programme.title: Timber Kings: Make a Mark
programme.desc: Bryan Jr. makes a mark in the self-proclaimed log home capital of the world. Back at the yard, Andre tries to control Peter when his pent-up creativity goes in overdrive.

programme.title: Timber Kings: The Getaway
programme.desc: As winter approaches, the rush to build two summer getaway homes is on. Peter leads his team in Alberta while Bryan Jr. and his guys head north to Alaska.

programme.title: Timber Kings: Great Expectations
programme.desc: In upstate New York, Beat builds three grandiose gazebos for two brothers with neighbouring properties. Back home, Bryan Jr. asks his son Little Man to take charge of his first build ever.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: Death In Inner Space
programme.desc: A scientist seeks a way to suspend bodily life functions during space travel, and his test subject dies from lack of oxygen. Accident or foul play?

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: The Talking Head
programme.desc: A 12 year old boy insists his dead father told him to kill his mother's new fiancée. But did the father truly die in an accidental plane crash?

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: The Devil Sells His Soul

programme.title: Deadly Women: Hearts of Stone

programme.title: Deadly Women

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Rochdale Groomers

programme.desc: Samantha has abdominal pains that make it difficult to function. Laura injures her shoulder in a workplace accident and is told that she is too young for the surgery needed to make her better.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Guns & Ice
programme.desc: The following day is Dog's birthday but that won't stop him from chasing a fugitive, who is facing firearms charges. Dog doesn't relish the thought of going after an alleged gunslinger on his birthday but he's never one to avoid a challenge.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: The Good Fight
programme.desc: Dog and Beth have to be at the state capitol bright and early to receive an award for their fight against ice in Hawaii. Afterwards, they're inspired to start the hunt for Shanna, an alleged ice user and identity thief with $80,000 in bonds.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: The Smackdown of Baby Lyssa
programme.desc: After meeting with the cosigner on a bond for a man named John, Dog and the Posse hatch a plan to lure him to a high rise apartment. But when night falls, their plan leaves a member of the team alone and in harm's way!

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: A Friend in Need
programme.desc: Roger has 27 prior arrests, spent years in jail and doesn't want to go back. But he's missed court and now Dog has to bring him in. To complicate matters, this fugitive lives in a tightly-knit community where good information won't come easy.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Your Lying Eyes
programme.desc: Kolani's on the run, left his wife to look after four kids and left Dog holding the bag for the $10k bond. So their pursuit on this case takes them into some parts of Ohau, and the human heart, that few of us will ever see.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Crime Don't Pay
programme.desc: Today the crew is hunting for Holly, a car thief. She's one of Tim's bonds and the entire Dog crew knows that means trouble - or at least a long and difficult chase.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Twisted Love
programme.desc: Dog is tracking down Cecilia, who's wanted for car theft, but she's nowhere to be found. Her girlfriend and co-signer has gone missing too. Just when the trail goes cold, a friend leads the team to the cosigner's empty apartment.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Where the Wild Things Are
programme.desc: Dog, Beth and the team are in Colorado and they face a special challenge - getting the kids to camp. After the drop-off, Dog is more than ready to track down a fugitive. Robin disposed of a body after a homicide, and now he's skipped his court date.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: No Luv Still
programme.desc: Dog is in search of a potentially dangerous fugitive, he's only 19 and charged with kidnapping and menacing. The case takes an unexpected turn when the Chapman clan ends up in a confrontation with the suspect's family.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Burger Commander
programme.desc: After visiting a popular burger restaurant, Willie and Jase get into a debate over which of them cooks the best burger, prompting them to challenge one another to a cooking contest.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: G.I. Si
programme.desc: When Willie and Jase argue over an old G.I. Joe toy, it leads them into a debate over who would make a better soldier. To settle the score, Willie and Jase head out to the woods with their friends and children for a paintball match.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Jase and the Argonauts
programme.desc: When Willie gets a soft serve machine for himself, he tells the guys they can't use it until they're done working. But when Jase thinks this is unfair, he takes matters into his own hands and finds a better object to use as motivation.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Fowl Playhouse
programme.desc: When Jase finds out Jep is planning to buy his kids a fancy playhouse from a catalogue, he and the guys decide to build one for Jep themselves. But the playhouse ends up looking more like a military-style duck blind.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: The Big LeCOWski
programme.desc: When Sadie spends the day at Duck Commander for a school project, the guys try to show her the ins-and-outs of how a successful company works. But when Willie questions Jase's ingenuity in creating new products they get into a debate.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: The Robertson family pulls together a family reunion to give Mia a big send-off before her surgery.

programme.desc: When the Governor of Louisiana announces that he will be coming to Duck Commander to present Willie with a commerce award, John Luke volunteers to give the introduction speech.

programme.title: Myth Hunters
programme.desc: Ron Wyatt, a Nashville nurse turned amateur archaeologist at 47, aimed to validate Bible stories. In 1977, he claimed finding Noah’s Ark on Mount Ararat and later dived for Egyptian chariot remains, supporting Moses’ Red Sea crossing.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Pterodactyls
programme.desc: Richard speaks with several explorers and cryptozoologists who believe pterodactyls, flying dinosaurs thought to have become extinct some 60 million years ago, are alive and thriving in remote places such as Papua New Guinea.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Richard witnesses a municipal employee in Toronto, Canada undergo a regression during which he re-experiences a previous life as a doctor in early 20 century England.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: NATURE’S MAGIC
programme.desc: Natural sites worldwide hold spiritual significance for civilizations, from ancient rituals to modern reverence, each crackling with unique energy and a hint of magic.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: MYSTERIOUS WORKS OF ART
programme.desc: Great art and its creators unveil mysteries more intricate than TV dramas. Amidst theft and intrigue, we persist in solving them. Yet, the truth often lies buried with those who created it, adding layers of intrigue to the art world.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: An investigation into the various conspiracy theories revolving around the tragic death of Diana, The People's Princess, in Paris in August 1997.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: What on Earth are they spraying? Richard travels into the field to meet several researchers who present compelling evidence that chemicals or biological agents are being deliberately sprayed into the atmosphere by some shadowy government agency.

programme.title: Myth Hunters
programme.desc: German adventurer Heinrich Schlieman embarks on a lifelong quest to find the mythical city of Troy and its fabulous treasures.

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: In 1911, a gun battle on the streets of London, The Siege of Sidney Street the first breaking-news story in history.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the American Museum of Natural History, New York
programme.desc: From a dinosaur embryo that solves a murder mystery to a spacecraft catching a comet’s tail, discover the secrets inside the American Museum of Natural History.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the Egyptian Museum
programme.desc: From the mystery of a Pharaoh’s gold to the strange practice of animal mummification, discover the secrets inside the Egyptian Museum.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside The ROM, Toronto
programme.desc: From a medieval hand-grenade to an extremely rare dinosaur, discover the secrets inside the Royal Ontario Museum.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the Louvre
programme.desc: From murdered kings to stolen masterpieces, discover the secrets inside the Louvre.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the National Archeological Museum of Athens
programme.desc: From a dodgy archeologist who discovers a golden mask to an ancient gadget that reveals the future, discover the secrets inside the National Archaeological Museum.

programme.title: scienceNOW: What Will the Future Be Like?
programme.desc: The technologies that will transform our lives decades from now are already taking shape in laboratories around the world. Segments include: Humanoid Robots; Mind-Reading Machines; Augmented Reality; Profile - Adrien Treuille.

programme.title: Naked Science: Making Mountains
programme.desc: The Rockies. Magnificent and awe-inspiring mysterious, the Rockies are one of the longest mountain ranges in the world. Naked Science investigates the geological processes that build these massive mountains.

programme.title: Naked Science: How To Avert an Asteroid Impact
programme.desc: Throughout history, the earth has been bombarded from space. Asteroid impacts have caused mass extinctions – including the dinosaur wipe out.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: HURRICANES
programme.desc: Hurricanes, Typhoons and Cyclones all the same thing with different names – what they also have in common is their destructive power! Hurricanes are large, swirling storms and winds from a hurricane can damage buildings or trees.

programme.title: Wonders: Can We Make Life?
programme.desc: New tools allow researchers to use cells to create and edit their own DNA with more ease and less cost than ever before. NOVA Wonders explores the benefits and the burden of risk surrounding this controversial new technology.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Water Worlds
programme.desc: The key ingredient to life in the universe is water. Until recently we thought Earth was the only place in the solar system to have any water at all. Now that probes are peering around nearby worlds we are finding that is just not the case.

programme.title: Trajectory - Milestones in Space Exploration
programme.desc: The Apollo Program Begins - At the very beginning of NASA's moon project things go wrong. Caves - ESA's special training program for astronauts. Planck - Mapping the cosmic microwave background radiation.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Landing on Mars
programme.desc: Mars sparks our curiosity like no other planet because it is so reachable. Despite having landed there, most of the information we gather is still done by instruments in the planet's orbit.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Tickets Please
programme.desc: No spacecraft have traveled so fast or so close to a star before. It is hoped to reveal the source of the solar winds that effect us here on Earth

programme.title: The New Frontier: From There To Here
programme.desc: Series introduction and the study of Earth from orbit.

programme.title: The New Frontier: GIANTS IN THE DARK
programme.desc: Asteroids, the debris from the early solar system hold tantalizing clues to planetary formation. The spacecraft Psyche readies to study a unique metallic asteroid.

programme.title: Trajectory - Milestones in Space Exploration
programme.desc: Cold War & The Start of the Space Race - Cold War rivalry and the acceleration of space exploration. Servicing Hubble - Preparations for Hubble's final servicing mission. Chasing a Comet - Launch and the difficult path to rendezvous.

programme.title: The New Frontier: The Ice Giants
programme.desc: Beyond the asteroid belt lay the ice giants, some can be seen with the naked eye, others only glimpsed once by a passing probe.

programme.title: Trajectory - Milestones in Space Exploration
programme.desc: Project Gemini Commences - Did Gemini give the US a lead in the space race? Venus Express - The last days of ESA's Venus probe. EMU - NASA's Extravehicular Mobility Unit. Deep Space Communications - ESA's worldwide network of giant dishes.

programme.title: Britain's Bloody Crown
programme.desc: Dan Jones tells then story of Richard Neville, Earl of Warwick.

programme.title: Britain's Bloody Crown
programme.desc: Did Richard III really kill the 'Princes in the Tower'?

programme.title: Britain's Great Cathedrals with Tony Robinson: York Minster
programme.desc: York Minster has survived fires, war and subsidence.

programme.title: Britain's Great Cathedrals with Tony Robinson: Canterbury Cathedral
programme.desc: Tony tells the story of Britain's oldest cathedral.

programme.title: Britain's Great Cathedrals with Tony Robinson: Durham Cathedral

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: The Bronx is burning, became the defining image of New York City in the 70s. Politicians used it as a backdrop illustrate the dire situation in America’s cities while local officials let it burn

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown: Mississippi Delta
programme.desc: Tony seeks to challenge his notions of Mississippi by taking a road trip to meet a cast of Mississippians enjoying the diverse foodways and embracing the traditional and contemporary music worlds.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: Tony travels to Mexico City and Oaxaca to meet who fight for the food, the art, and the lives they believe in. In the Santa Anita neighbourhood of Mexico City, Tony meets boxer Jorge Lacierva.

programme.title: Arthur: MacFrensky/The Good The Bad and the Binky
programme.desc: Cheating comes in to play when Francine and Brain are locked in a stiff competition to win student of the month. Babysitter Binky introduces his charge to the world of pro-wrestling, but is he being a good role model?

programme.title: Arthur: Lend Me Your Ear/The Butler Did It
programme.desc: Arthur, Buster, and Brain are worried when Mr. Ratburn isn't acting like himself. He's always pleased to see them, so what’s made him change? When Mr. Crosswire gets a robot butler for Father's Day, Muffy fears Bailey may be replaced.

programme.title: Arthur: Binky Vs. Binky/Operation: DW!
programme.desc: Binky may be tough and strong, but when it comes to sports, he always seems to choke. D.W. is having trouble hearing and needs an ear operation. Is it okay to be just a little bit scared?

programme.title: Arthur: Fifteen
programme.desc: How much can Arthur and his friends do in 15 minutes? Can they all accomplish these missions impossible before the clock counts all the way down? Only time will tell!

programme.title: Arthur: How The Cookie Crumbles/Sue Ellen's Littler Sister
programme.desc: What's a budding entrepreneur to do when her homemade cookies hit the big time? Especially when the recipe isn't really hers? Then, Arthur and Francine would love to lend their sisters to Sue Ellen, who's sick of being an only child.

programme.title: Arthur: Brain's Brain/Brain Sees Stars
programme.desc: Brain's Easter egg hunts are notoriously hard. But this year's might prove impossible if Brain can't remember where he hid the final egg. Then, crazy coincidences have prevented Brain from seeing a meteor shower in person.

programme.title: Arthur: D.W., All Fired Up/I'd Rather Read It Myself
programme.desc: When Ms. Morgan announces they'll be having a fire drill during preschool, D.W. wants no part of it--it sounds way too scary. Then, the Tibble Twins are better than D.W. at just about everything.

programme.title: Arthur: Portrait of the Artist as a Young Tibble/The Secret Guardians
programme.desc: The Tibble Twins start a new business to earn enough money to buy the coveted Krummy Kreepy Kastle. When and friends stumble upon a beautiful woodland oasis right by their homes, they vow to tell no one and keep it as their secret spot.

programme.title: Military Machines: America in World War II
programme.desc: The Marines are America's fighting elite, a small police force that grew into one of the most feared forces in the Pacific War.

programme.title: Military Machines: America in World War II
programme.desc: This is the story of the creation of the Allied military from the small 100,000-man force of the post World War I era to the mighty military industrial complex that fought fascist forces around the world. 10 Hour Series

programme.title: Military Machines: America in World War II
programme.desc: The US Coast Guard was the first branch of the US military to see action in WWII.

programme.title: Military Machines: America in World War II
programme.desc: The US Coast Guard saw extensive action throughout the Second World War, battling U-boats within sight of mainland America.

programme.title: War Wrecks: The Untold Stories: The Lost Tanks of D-Day
programme.desc: In Normandy, June 6, 1944, a dozen Sherman tanks equipped with floats, the famous Duplex Drives (DD), leave their ship to swim to Omaha Beach... not all of them make it.

programme.title: War Wrecks: The Untold Stories: The Last Flight of the B-17
programme.desc: Twenty-six metres beneath the waves at the foot of the majestic Calvi Citadel in Corsica lies an American plane from the Second World War.

programme.title: History Of Weapons: Ranged Weapons
programme.desc: Ranged weapons guarantee a warrior's best chance of survival. International experts survey history’s most important ranged weapons, from the Roman spear and the English longbow, right up to the futuristic railgun.

programme.title: Hitler's Secret Bomb: Episode 1
programme.desc: Did Nazi uranium make it to Japan in the final months of WW2? Were the Axis powers closer to a bomb than we think? Drama unfolds as experts shed new light on the hardships, challenges and secrets of a war-torn, pre-nuclear world.

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: Destroyers and Cruisers showcases ships that with their speed and firepower became the backbones of 20th century navies. We tell the incredible story of USS Laffey, the WW2 destroyer that would come to be known as ‘the ship that would not die’.

programme.title: Secret Wars Uncovered: Greece
programme.desc: Greece: In 1944, 28 Greek socialists were shot in Syntagma Square and 100,000 had perished by the end of the Civil War. Was this the old order Winston Churchill had longed for?

programme.desc: Baby Bop is sad because she lost her pet caterpillar, Monty. Barney and the others try to help her find it. Baby Bop is surprised to discover Monty inside a chrysalis. Everyone is excited to find Monty—now a beautiful butterfly!

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Once Upon a Fairy Tale
programme.desc: Mario is trying to write his very own fairy tale. The children help by acting out some of their favorite fairy tales to give Mario some ideas.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Playing Games - No, No, No!
programme.desc: Baby Bop has trouble following the rules and taking turns while playing games. Barney and Riff help her learn how to follow the rules. In the end, Baby Bop and BJ have fun playing together, and Baby Bop learns that rules make games fun for everyone!

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Gift Of The Dinos - Winter
programme.desc: It's Christmas and BJ dreams of getting a new baseball glove to go with his baseball, while Riff hopes for new drumsticks to use his drum. Barney reminds them that Christmas is more than just getting gifts and helps them learn the meaning of Christmas.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: The Park Kids decide to have a Dog Show to show off their pets. A rainy day has everyone stuck in the Caboose. Barney suggests fun activities.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: The Big Garden - Get Happy
programme.desc: Baby Bop wants Barney, BJ and the kids to play with her instead of working in their garden. They promise to play with her as soon as they finish. Baby Bop can't wait, and tries many ways to get them to come play with her now, none of which work.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Sharing Is Caring!
programme.desc: The children bring toys to donate to Mr. Boyd's toy drive. Baby Bop and BJ arrive and after some convincing, Baby Bop learns about the merits of sharing.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Play Piano With Me!
programme.desc: The children learn all about the piano when they play games, listen to real pianos and even play a little themselves.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Home Safe Home
